verses.text: This is the thing thou shalt do to hallow them: take one young bullock, two rams without blemish, unleavened bread, cakes tempered with oil, wafers anointed with oil — of wheaten flour. Put them in one basket; bring them with the bullock and rams. Guidance: Consecration is costly. One bullock and two rams without blemish, three kinds of bread — the whole basket is the price of being set apart for God. Holiness costs the best of the flock.

Bring Aaron and his sons to the tabernacle door; wash them. Put the garments on Aaron — coat, robe, ephod, breastplate, girdle. Set the mitre with the holy crown. Pour the anointing oil. Bring his sons; coat, gird, bonnet them. Consecrate them; the priesthood is theirs forever. Guidance: Washed, clothed, anointed — the priest is changed from outside in. Hebrews ties this to Christ: washed by water, clothed in righteousness, anointed by His Spirit.

Bring the bullock to the tabernacle; Aaron and his sons lay hands on its head. Slay before the LORD. Put blood on the altar's horns with thy finger; pour the rest at its base. Burn fat, kidneys, caul. Flesh, skin, dung burn outside the camp — a sin offering. Guidance: Hands on the head transfer guilt; the animal dies in their place. Hebrews reads this in Jesus — who suffered outside the gate to sanctify His people by His blood.

Take one ram; Aaron and sons lay hands on its head. Slay; sprinkle blood round about the altar. Cut the ram in pieces; wash inwards and legs; put with the pieces and head. Burn the whole ram on the altar — a burnt offering, sweet savor unto the LORD. Guidance: The whole animal is given — nothing held back. The burnt offering is total surrender, a sweet savor rising to God. To minister, one must first be wholly given.

The ram of consecration: Aaron and sons lay hands. Slay; put blood on the right earlobe, thumb, big toe of Aaron and sons. Sprinkle round the altar. Mix altar blood with anointing oil; sprinkle on Aaron, sons, garments. Take fat, kidneys, caul, right shoulder, cakes. Wave before the LORD; burn — consecration, sweet savor. Guidance: Blood on ear, thumb, toe — what he hears, does, and goes is set apart. Holiness reaches to the smallest joint.

Take the breast of the ram of consecration; wave it — it is thy portion. Sanctify the breast and shoulder for Aaron and his sons. This is a statute forever from the children of Israel — from their peace offerings, their heave offering to the LORD. Guidance: The priest does not work for nothing. The breast and shoulder of Israel's peace offerings are his by law. Those who serve at the altar live by the altar.

The holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons' after him, to be anointed in them and consecrated in them. The son that is priest in his stead shall put them on seven days when he comes into the tabernacle to minister in the holy place. Guidance: Priesthood is handed down with the garments. Seven days of wearing them shapes the new priest. Some callings come with a robe that has held holier shoulders before.

The holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons' after him, to be anointed in them and consecrated in them. The son that is priest in his stead shall put them on seven days when he comes into the tabernacle to minister in the holy place. Guidance: Priesthood is handed down with the garments. Boil the ram of consecration in the holy place. Aaron and sons eat the flesh and bread at the tabernacle door. They eat what made atonement, to consecrate them. A stranger shall not eat — they are holy. If aught remain to morning, burn it; it shall not be eaten. Guidance: A meal is part of consecration. The priests eat what atoned for them. God's holiness includes a table — but no leftovers; what is holy is not stored for casual reuse.

Do thus to Aaron and his sons seven days. Offer a bullock daily as sin offering, for atonement; cleanse and anoint the altar. Seven days atone for the altar and sanctify it. The altar shall be most holy; whatsoever toucheth it shall be holy. Guidance: Even the altar needs cleansing. Seven days of repeated atonement before it is itself holy. God's call to ministry takes patience to receive; you don't rush ordination.

Offer two lambs daily — one morning, one at even. With each: a tenth of flour with quarter-hin oil; quarter-hin wine for drink offering. A continual burnt offering throughout your generations — at the tabernacle door. There I will meet you and speak with thee. Guidance: Morning and evening, a lamb and bread and wine, every day forever. Worship is rhythmed, not occasional. The pattern would later teach Israel to recognize Jesus.

There I will meet with Israel; the tabernacle shall be sanctified by My glory. I will sanctify the tabernacle, altar, Aaron, and his sons. I will dwell among Israel and be their God. They shall know I am the LORD their God who brought them out of Egypt — that I may dwell among them. Guidance: God did not deliver Egypt to leave Israel in the desert. He brought them out so He could move in. The exodus has been an address change.
